claim: Andrej Karpathy estimates 10+ years to AGI, contrasting with market hype claiming 2025 is the year of AGI; decade of agents needed for scaffolding buildout
actionable: When planning long-term AI product strategy, assume 5-10 year timelines for agent maturity rather than single-year breakthroughs; focus on scaffolding infrastructure

claim: Core model capabilities lag behind required scaffolding; integration work, sensor/actuator bridges, and safety work remain as major unsolved problems
actionable: When evaluating AI capability claims, assess entire system including infrastructure, integration, sensors, and safety measures, not just core model benchmarks

claim: LLMs memorize more than generalize; animals learn through evolution-pre-packaged knowledge while LLMs rely on next-token prediction from training data
actionable: When designing LLM evaluation or improvement, focus on generalization metrics (Arc, reasoning tasks) rather than benchmarks that reward memorization

claim: Reinforcement learning suffers from outcome-based reward problems: intermediate reasoning steps get incorrectly credited/penalized based on final answer
actionable: When training with RL on LLM reasoning, use process rewards or intermediate supervision instead of outcome-only rewards to avoid penalizing correct reasoning

claim: System prompt learning enables learning without parameter changes; explicit remembering in system prompt more effective than fine-tuning for adaptation
actionable: When adapting LLMs to new domains, prefer updating system prompt with learned strategies over full fine-tuning for flexibility and reversibility

claim: Claude's 17000-word system message provides general problem-solving knowledge including counting steps and explicit enumeration techniques
actionable: When building specialized LLM systems, invest in comprehensive system prompts with explicit problem-solving strategies relevant to target tasks

claim: Cognitive core: small model optimized for generalization by sacrificing encyclopedic knowledge; on-device as kernel of LLM personal computing
actionable: Design on-device LLM systems around small general-purpose models paired with external knowledge sources rather than large memorization-heavy models

claim: Agents not ready for fully autonomous 20-30 minute tasks; prefer collaborative iterative chunks with explanations and API verification
actionable: When deploying coding agents in production, require intermediate review checkpoints; don't trust fully autonomous long-running agent tasks

claim: Mountains of slop: unsupervised agent output of thousands of LOC without detailed review creates unmaintainable code
actionable: When building agentic coding systems, establish human review requirements proportional to output size; prevent fully autonomous multi-hour agent runs
